tipRules.tip: KVD: 'High muddy water = spinnerbait time. Use bulky spinnerbait with Colorado blades — more vibration helps fish find it, provides lift, requires slower wind for more time in strike zone. Bright colors: fire tiger, chartreuse, fluorescent orange/yellow, pearl white. Black works too for silhouette in dirty water.'

KVD: 'When debris prevents spinnerbait fishing in muddy water, switch to a 1/2oz flipping jig in black/blue with blue craw Rage Craw trailer. Flip into thick cover or swim it depending on the area. White jig is another option.'

knowledge.insight: KVD: 'Rising water — bass push shallow and spread out. Best spring days can occur when warming water rises fast. Key on runoff areas: backs of creeks, culverts, residential yards, city street drainage. Water temp can vary 5 degrees between pockets — find the warmest runoff. Don't overlook ultra-shallow water — bass will put noses on seawalls and banks in inches of water.'

KVD: 'Cold + rising + muddy is the toughest spring combo. Bass in winter pattern are less likely to move to prespawn areas. Fish a flat-sided crankbait that displaces a lot of water, close to bottom or near objects. Make parallel casts to channel swings, steep banks, and riprap. Some fish always live shallow — target them.'
